spec_match.rationale: Demonstrates the exact MIME-filtering pattern the spec encourages: read `dataTransfer.items[0].type` inside `beforeinput`, compare against an allow-list, and call `event.preventDefault()` on miss.

interactive.rationale: Five MIME toggle checkboxes drive a live `acceptedTypes` set; five simulate buttons fire a synthetic `beforeinput`; a real-time log records accept/reject decisions.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb promises a toggle-driven gating demo with logged accept/reject decisions; the page delivers that.

edge_cases.rationale: Filter-by-type is exercised, but no demo of `dataTransfer.items[0].type === ''` (some IMEs commit empty type and rely on the file extension), no multi-item arrays, and no `accept everything matching image/*` wildcard case.

design_system.rationale: Variables for colour, button colour overrides paired with background, but the accept/reject log lines use `✓` / `✗` glyphs instead of SVG.

openQuestions.title: Add chromestatus reference link
openQuestions.detail: see-also is missing chromestatus.com/feature/5484282563919872 — required by the self-heal invariant.

openQuestions.title: Replace ✓ / ✗ log glyphs with inline SVG
openQuestions.detail: Convention is inline SVG with `currentColor`. Replace the two glyphs with small SVGs sized to the log font.

openQuestions.title: Handle empty-type and wildcard cases
openQuestions.detail: Some Android IMEs commit a file with `item.type === ''`. The current filter rejects it because it's not in the explicit list, which is unhelpful — the visitor doesn't see this happen. Add an `accept empty type` toggle and an `image/*` wildcard option to make the filter contract complete.

openQuestions.title: Show what a real IME commits
openQuestions.detail: Pair the synthetic buttons with a short table that lists the MIME types Android IMEs typically commit per sticker keyboard (GIPHY: image/gif; SwiftKey: image/png; Gboard stickers: image/webp). Helps developers calibrate their allow-list.

openQuestions.title: Demo a deny-list pattern
openQuestions.detail: Some apps prefer `accept all image/* except video/* and image/svg+xml` (svg is an XSS surface). Show that pattern explicitly — important for any app accepting untrusted IME input.

summary: Compact, focused gating demo with a real event log. Two simple follow-ups would round it out: add the chromestatus link and surface the empty-type / wildcard cases that IMEs in the wild actually produce.
